name: setFlagShow
description: Shows or hides the Zodiacal Light rendering. The Zodiacal Light is a faint, diffuse glow along the ecliptic plane, caused by sunlight scattering off interplanetary dust. It is best visible after sunset or before sunrise in very dark skies.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: `true` to show the Zodiacal Light, `false` to hide it.

alternative: Use `core.getStelProperty('ZodiacalLight.flagZodiacalLightDisplayed')` to read the current state.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use. The Zodiacal Light feature was introduced in Stellarium 0.14.0.

name: getFlagShow
description: Returns whether the Zodiacal Light is currently displayed.
returnDescription: `true` if the Zodiacal Light is visible, `false` otherwise.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setIntensity
description: Sets the intensity (brightness) of the Zodiacal Light rendering. Values above 1.0 make it appear brighter, below 1.0 dimmer.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getIntensity
description: Returns the current intensity (brightness) of the Zodiacal Light rendering.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var intensity = ZodiacalLight.getIntensity();
core.debug("Zodiacal Light intensity: " + intensity);

name: setColor
description: Sets the color tint for the Zodiacal Light rendering. White `(1, 1, 1)` gives natural colors (sunlight); other values create color casts.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values between 0.0 and 1.0.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getColor
description: Returns the current color tint applied to the Zodiacal Light rendering.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var color = ZodiacalLight.getColor();
core.debug("Zodiacal Light color: RGB(" + color[0] + ", " + color[1] + ", " + color[2] + ")");
